title: Bolbitis longiflagellata
taxonomy: Bolbitis longiflagellata is a species of fern in the family Lomariopsidaceae. This plant is native to tropical regions and is often found growing near water. The classification of Bolbitis longiflagellata is further broken down into its kingdom, as a plant, it falls under the kingdom Plantae, and at the rank of species, it is classified as a member of the genus Bolbitis. The specific classification of Bolbitis longiflagellata is as follows: kingdom - Plantae, clade - angiosperms, clade - monocots, clade - magnoliids, class - Polypodiopsida, order - Polypodiales, family - Lomariopsidaceae, genus - Bolbitis, and species - B. longiflagellata.

intro: Bolbitis longiflagellata is a species of fern, specifically a type of water fern that belongs to the family Lomariopsidaceae. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects, making it a potential treatment for various health conditions such as wounds, infections, and oxidative stress. The bioactive constituents of Bolbitis longiflagellata include flavonoids, alkaloids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. Bolbitis longiflagellata is often used to make herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are traditionally used to treat fever, cough, and respiratory issues.
